Romanian footballer

Vasile Stan

**Vasile Stan** (born 11 February 1945) is a [Romanian](/wiki/Romania "Romania") former football [goalkeeper](/wiki/Goalkeeper_(association_football) "Goalkeeper (association football)").[[3]](#cite_note-WF-4)[[4]](#cite_note-5)[[5]](#cite_note-6)[[6]](#cite_note-7)

## Playing career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Vasile_Stan&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Playing career")]

Stan was born on 11 February 1945 in [Bucharest](/wiki/Bucharest "Bucharest"), Romania and began playing football at the age of 15 under the guidance of coach Lăzărescu at [fourth league](/wiki/Liga_IV "Liga IV") side F.C.M.E.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-arges-2) In 1966 he went to play for [Dinamo Victoria București](/wiki/Victoria_Bucure%C8%99ti "Victoria București") in [Divizia B](/wiki/Liga_II "Liga II").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-arges-2)

One year later, Stan switched teams again, joining [Argeș Pitești](/wiki/FC_Arge%C8%99_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Argeș Pitești") where he made his [Divizia A](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I") debut under coach Ion Bălănescu on 24 September 1967, keeping a clean sheet in a 4–0 home win over [Dinamo Bacău](/wiki/FCM_Bac%C4%83u "FCM Bacău").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-arges-2) He played only four games in his first top-league season, as [Narcis Coman](/wiki/Narcis_Coman "Narcis Coman") was preferred as first-choice goalkeeper.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-arges-2) Thus, he went to play for [Jiul Petroșani](/wiki/CSM_Jiul_Petro%C8%99ani "CSM Jiul Petroșani") where he worked with coach [Titus Ozon](/wiki/Titus_Ozon "Titus Ozon"), being first-choice for three seasons.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-arges-2)[[7]](#cite_note-8) Afterwards he returned to Argeș where in his [first season](/wiki/1971%E2%80%9372_Divizia_A "1971–72 Divizia A") coaches Ozon and [Florin Halagian](/wiki/Florin_Halagian "Florin Halagian") used him in 29 games from a total of 30, helping the club win its first title.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-arges-2)[[8]](#cite_note-jurnalul-9)[[9]](#cite_note-10) Subsequently, he appeared in four games in the [1972–73 European Cup](/wiki/1972%E2%80%9373_European_Cup "1972–73 European Cup") campaign, eliminating [Aris Bonnevoie](/wiki/FC_Aris_Bonnevoie "FC Aris Bonnevoie") in the first round, keeping clean sheets in both legs.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[10]](#cite_note-WF2-11)[[11]](#cite_note-12) In the following round they won a home game with 2–1 against [Real Madrid](/wiki/Real_Madrid_CF "Real Madrid CF"), but lost the second leg with 3–1 in which he conceded two goals from [Santillana](/wiki/Santillana_(footballer) "Santillana (footballer)") and one from [Toni Grande](/wiki/Toni_Grande "Toni Grande").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[8]](#cite_note-jurnalul-9)[[10]](#cite_note-WF2-11)[[12]](#cite_note-13) Stan also played in the first leg in the first round of the [1973–74 UEFA Cup](/wiki/1973%E2%80%9374_UEFA_Cup "1973–74 UEFA Cup"), conceding five goals in the 5–1 loss to [Fenerbahçe](/wiki/Fenerbah%C3%A7e_S.K._(football) "Fenerbahçe S.K. (football)").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[13]](#cite_note-14) In 1975 he went to play one season for [Politehnica Iași](/wiki/FC_Politehnica_Ia%C8%99i_(1945) "FC Politehnica Iași (1945)") where he worked with coach [Ilie Oană](/wiki/Ilie_Oan%C4%83 "Ilie Oană"), making his last Divizia A appearance on 20 June 1976 in a 3–1 home win over [FC Constanța](/wiki/FC_Farul_Constan%C8%9Ba "FC Farul Constanța"), totaling 204 games in which he conceded 227 goals.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-arges-2)

Stan ended his career at [Muscelul Câmpulung](/wiki/ACS_Muscelul_C%C3%A2mpulung "ACS Muscelul Câmpulung") where his former Argeș teammate, [Ion Barbu](/wiki/Ion_Barbu_(footballer,_born_1938) "Ion Barbu (footballer, born 1938)") was coach, and he was also colleagues with other former Argeș teammates such as [Constantin Radu](/wiki/Constantin_Radu_(footballer) "Constantin Radu (footballer)"), [Radu Jercan](/wiki/Radu_Jercan "Radu Jercan") and [Marian Popescu](/wiki/Marian_Popescu "Marian Popescu").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-arges-2) They managed to get the team promoted from the [third league](/wiki/Liga_III "Liga III") to the second.[[2]](#cite_note-arges-2)

## Coaching career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Vasile_Stan&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Coaching career")]

After he ended his playing career, Stan worked as a coach, first as an assistant at [Dacia Pitești](/wiki/FC_Dacia_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Dacia Pitești"), and later as a head coach for the same team.[[2]](#cite_note-arges-2) He also was head coach at IPA Slatina and [Muscelul Câmpulung](/wiki/ACS_Muscelul_C%C3%A2mpulung "ACS Muscelul Câmpulung").[[2]](#cite_note-arges-2) He was the assistant coach of [Nicolae Dobrin](/wiki/Nicolae_Dobrin "Nicolae Dobrin") and [Constantin Cârstea](/wiki/Constantin_C%C3%A2rstea "Constantin Cârstea") at [Argeș Pitești](/wiki/FC_Arge%C8%99_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Argeș Pitești").[[2]](#cite_note-arges-2) Stan was also the team's head coach for short periods of time, first in 2002 and second in 2006 when he coached for the last five games of the season after [Sorin Cârțu](/wiki/Sorin_C%C3%A2r%C8%9Bu "Sorin Cârțu") was dismissed.[[3]](#cite_note-WF-4)[[2]](#cite_note-arges-2)[[14]](#cite_note-15)

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Vasile_Stan&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Honours")]

**Argeș Pitești**

* [Divizia A](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I"): [1971–72](/wiki/1971%E2%80%9372_Divizia_A "1971–72 Divizia A")[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)

**Muscelul Câmpulung**

* [Divizia C](/wiki/Liga_III "Liga III"): [1976–77](/wiki/1976%E2%80%9377_Divizia_C "1976–77 Divizia C")[[2]](#cite_note-arges-2)
